meta.name: Luke Quarderer
meta.title: Engineering Portfolio
meta.intro: Selected projects from my engineering degree — designed, built, tested, and documented. Each entry lists the skills applied, the tools used, and the artefacts produced as evidence of the work.
meta.aboutBio:
I am a second-year student at the University of Michigan, pursuing Aerospace Engineering at the College of Engineering. I am particularly interested in the application of aerospace technologies within the defense sector. I am eager to build a strong technical foundation through coursework, hands-on projects, and collaborative experiences, and I look forward to exploring opportunities where engineering innovation supports real-world defense challenges.

I currently reside in Canberra, Australia as a United States expat. I have lived 6 years as a diplomat overseas in London and Canberra experiencing this intricate environment that encompasses the intersection of government, military, and diplomacy.

Recently, I have completed an internship with the defense company Leidos aiding in data management and automation solutions utilised by more than 45 members a part of the Sea Archer program. I am eager to continue learning through coursework, but especially through these sorts of industry experiences.

I love to travel and add new countries to my list (I backpacked Colombia and Vanuatu this year). As of now I have been to 31 different countries and play to tick off the Dominican Republic as my 32nd.
